channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California man who shot 2 kindergarteners had lengthy criminal history, mental health issues: police
item.description: The gunman in California that shot and critically injured two kindergartners is believed to have suffered from significant mental health issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Foreign minister says in interview that Russia will use 'all means' to defend its interests
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s top diplomat said in an interview with former Fox News host Tucker Carlson released Friday that Moscow doesn't want a war with the United States but will use “all means” to defend its interests. Sergey Lavrov argued that while Russia and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Investigators start small, focus on family in search for motive
item.description: Former law enforcement officers detailed the methods that the NYPD is likely using in their active manhunt for UnitedHealth CEO Brian Thompson's assassin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO heads into third day as new clues emerge
item.description: As the investigation into a masked gunman who stalked and killed the head of one of the largest U.S. health insurers moves into its third day, possible leads have emerged about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo, Jake Offenhartz And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Slaying of UnitedHealthcare CEO spotlights complex challenge companies face in protecting top brass
item.description: In an era when online anger and social tensions are increasingly directed at the businesses consumers count on, Meta last year spent $24.4 million to surround CEO Mark Zuckerberg with security.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Cathy Bussewitz And Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New clues emerged Thursday in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 186 minors cited in Utah bar sting
item.description: Over 180 citations were issued to minors for being inside a Sugar House bar following an investigation into concerns of "serious violations" of Utah's alcohol laws.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey leaders speak to DHS as unusual drone sightings now also reported over New York
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy spoke with federal and state officials about the sightings of drones in recent weeks, but said there was no threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Photos show suspected killer's face after shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: Police on Thursday shared new images of the suspected shooter in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: Dollar Tree could change prices, remove merchandise amid Trump's tariff plan
item.description: Dollar Tree announced they are ready to make certain changes amid President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest publishing launch of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, Fox 5

item.title: New LAPD chief says he will work to protect immigrants ahead of Trump's plans for mass deportations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The new chief of the Los Angeles police force said his department is working with consulate offices for Mexico and other Central American countries to draft a plan to protect immigrants ahead of the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police test DNA and fingerprints on discarded bottle as they hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer
item.description: A masked man who fatally shot the CEO of one of the nation’s largest health insurers in a targeted attack remained at large a day later.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: A list of mispronounced words provides a retrospective of 2024, from Kamala to Chappell
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and breakout pop star Chappell Roan were among the year's most talked-about people. Their names were also among the most mispronounced.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Community 'college deserts' leave students stranded from higher education
item.description: College deserts have gained more attention in recent years as they are considered a significant reason some groups are not attending college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: British rock band robbed at gunpoint in California, group says
item.description: A British indie rock band that had just embarked on a U.S. tour said they were robbed at gunpoint outside a Starbucks in California on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KTLA 5

item.title: Frontier Airlines to roll out first-class seating, other perks in 2025
item.description: Frontier Airlines will offer first-class seating for the first time in its history starting in late 2025, the company announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, ABC 4

item.title: Trump may replace Hegseth with DeSantis: WSJ
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is rumored to be considering replacing his Defense Secretary pick, former Fox News personality Pete Hegseth, with Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: National Cookie Day 2024 deals
item.description: Cookie lovers come together today to celebrate National Cookie Day. Here are a few stores celebrating.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's onions is over: CDC
item.description: An outbreak across 14 states in October connected to McDonald’s slivered onions from the popular menu item impacted at least 100 people.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trial begins in the killing of an Ole Miss student who was known in the local LGBTQ+ community
item.description: A University of Mississippi student who disappeared in 2022 was a happy person who spoke with his mother daily and called her every year to sing happy birthday, his mom testified Tuesday in the trial of the man charged with killing her son.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US closes investigation into E. coli outbreak linked to onions in McDonald's Quarter Pounders
item.description: The U.S. government said Tuesday it has closed its investigation into an E. coli outbreak tied to McDonald's Quarter Pounder hamburgers after determining there is no longer a safety risk. The outbreak, which was first reported Oct.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Great Lakes region braces for more snow while cleaning up after lake-effect storms
item.description: Even more snow could be on the way for places in the Great Lakes region that are still digging out after days of storms caused deadly wrecks, collapsed a barn on top of 100 cows and buried some towns under nearly 6 feet (1.8 meters).
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
